title: NSF 24-520: Long-Term Ecological Research (LTER)
description: To address ecological questions that cannot be resolved with short-term observations or experiments, NSF established the Long-Term Ecological Research Program (LTER) in 1980. Research at LTER sites emphasizes the study of ecological phenomena over long periods of time. The program supports research on ecosystem processes, climate change impacts, biodiversity, and sustainability. Sites are chosen to represent major ecosystem types.

title: NSF 24-525: Computer and Network Systems Research
description: This solicitation invites proposals for research on computer systems, networking, cybersecurity, and privacy. Topics include machine learning applications, algorithmic innovations, network protocols, security vulnerabilities, and data protection mechanisms. The program supports both fundamental discoveries and applied research with real-world impact on technology development.

title: NSF 24-530: Education and Workforce Development in STEM
description: Supporting education research, curriculum development, and professional training programs to broaden participation in STEM fields. Focus areas include K-12 education improvements, undergraduate degree programs, graduate training, and workforce development initiatives. Special emphasis on reaching underrepresented and underserved populations including minorities, women, and rural communities.

title: NIH R01 Grant: Biomedical Research and Development
description: The NIH Research Project Grant (R01) supports discrete, specified, circumscribed research projects in all areas of health-related research. This includes clinical investigations, biomedical research, health services research, and behavioral studies. Projects may focus on disease mechanisms, treatment development, prevention strategies, or health disparities. Eligible institutions include universities, medical schools, and research centers.

title: NIH R21 Grant: Exploratory Research in Public Health
description: The Exploratory/Developmental Research Grant (R21) supports innovative, high-risk biomedical and behavioral research projects that are exploratory and novel. Unlike traditional R01 grants, R21 grants allow for higher-risk, hypothesis-generating research on emerging health problems. Eligible researchers include faculty at universities and medical schools conducting health-related research.

title: NSF 24-535: Social, Behavioral, and Economic Sciences
description: Research program supporting investigations in psychology, sociology, economics, political science, anthropology, and related social sciences. Areas of focus include human behavior, social systems, economic decision-making, and community dynamics. Special interest in research with applications to equity, inclusion, and diversity in communities. Proposals may integrate behavioral and computational approaches.

title: NSF 24-540: Engineering Research and Graduate Education
description: Supports engineering research across multiple disciplines: mechanical systems, civil infrastructure, electrical systems, chemical engineering, and materials. Programs emphasize innovation in manufacturing, robotics, autonomous systems, and sustainable engineering solutions. Welcomes interdisciplinary proposals combining engineering with computer science, environmental science, or biomedical applications. Strong emphasis on workforce development through graduate research assistantships.

title: NIH NCI Cancer Biology and Prevention Research
description: The National Cancer Institute supports cancer research including prevention, detection, treatment, and survivorship studies. Research areas encompass molecular mechanisms of cancer, drug development, immunotherapy, precision medicine approaches, and health disparities in cancer care. Emphasis on translating basic discoveries into clinical applications and reducing cancer burden in underserved populations.

title: NSF 24-545: Materials Research and Discovery
description: Solicitation for research on design, synthesis, characterization, and processing of novel materials. Covers metals, polymers, ceramics, composites, biomaterials, and quantum materials. Applications include energy storage, sustainable manufacturing, biocompatible devices, and nanotechnology. Encourages computational modeling, machine learning for materials discovery, and high-throughput experimental methods.

title: NIH SBIR Phase II: Small Business Research and Development
description: Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) program for small businesses to conduct high-risk, high-reward research in biomedical sciences and health technologies. Phase II supports commercialization-ready projects demonstrating feasibility from Phase I. Topics include medical devices, diagnostics, therapeutic approaches, and digital health solutions addressing unmet clinical needs.

title: NSF 24-550: Robotics and Autonomous Systems
description: Research on robotic systems, autonomous vehicles, human-robot interaction, and intelligent automation. Applications span manufacturing, healthcare delivery in remote areas, disaster response, and accessibility for elderly and disabled populations. Emphasis on machine learning for perception and control, collaborative robotics, and socially responsible AI design.

title: NIH NIEHS Environmental Health and Toxicology Research
description: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences supports research on environmental exposures, occupational health, sustainable chemicals, and environmental justice. Application domains include air quality, water contamination, radiation exposure, and chemical health hazards. Special focus on vulnerable populations experiencing disproportionate environmental burden and climate-related health risks.
